title: lotus corniculatus subsp. preslii
intro: Lotus corniculatus subsp. preslii, also known as Bird's-foot trefoil or Lotus preslii, is a perennial legume plant in the subfamily Faboideae, known for its bright yellow flowers and ability to thrive in poor soil conditions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Lotus corniculatus subsp. preslii include its potential to reduce symptoms of menopausal hot flashes, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the leaves and flowers to be used in teas or capsules.
